evals.prompt: I need to automate opening the Settings app on my Android phone and navigating to Wi-Fi settings. Give me the exact u2cli commands to run. Assume the project is already set up (uv sync done).
evals.expected_output: A sequence of u2cli shell commands using app-start, wait, and click to open Settings and navigate to Wi-Fi/Network settings, using .venv/bin/u2cli prefix.

evals.prompt: I'm trying to log into my app com.example.myapp on Android. The login screen has a username field with resource-id 'com.example.myapp:id/et_username', a password field 'com.example.myapp:id/et_password', and a login button with text 'Sign In'. Give me a complete u2cli script to launch the app fresh (clear state), wait for the login screen, enter credentials, and tap Sign In. My test device serial is emulator-5554.
evals.expected_output: A complete sequence of u2cli commands targeting emulator-5554: app-start with --wait --stop (or app-clear first), wait for login screen, click username field, send-keys for username, click password field, send-keys for password, click 'Sign In'. Commands should use -s emulator-5554 or ANDROID_SERIAL.

evals.prompt: My Android app has a long scrollable list and I need to scroll down until I find an item called 'Advanced Settings', then click it. Also, before doing this I need to take a screenshot to see what's on the screen. How do I do this with u2cli?
evals.expected_output: Commands for: screenshot, then scroll --scrollable --to-text 'Advanced Settings', then click --text 'Advanced Settings'. May include dump-hierarchy to inspect the list first.

evals.prompt: How do I set up the project to use u2cli? I just cloned the AutoQA-Android repo and need to get u2cli working on my machine.
evals.expected_output: Instructions to run 'uv sync' to create .venv and install dependencies, then use .venv/bin/u2cli or activate the venv first. Should mention the pyproject.toml defines uiautomator2-cli as a dependency.
